topic.name: Historical Significance of Locations
topic.description: The documents discuss the historical importance and cultural heritage of specific locations and artifacts.

Document 1: Bedford Castle was a large medieval castle in Bedford, England.
The castle played a prominent part in both the civil war of the Anarchy and the First Barons' War.
Document 2: Washington Park in Albany, New York is the city's premier park and the site of many festivals and gatherings.
Due to the historical and architectural significance of the park and neighborhood both were included in 1972 as the Washington Park Historic District.

topic.name: Natural Species and Their Uses
topic.description: This topic encompasses various natural species, including fungi, plants, and animals, and their historical significance, characteristics, and uses in different contexts.

Document 1: Mycena californiensis is a species of fungus in the Mycenaceae family.
It grows saprobically, feeding on the fallen leaves and acorns of various oak species.
Document 2: The Giant Schnauzer is a working breed of dog developed in the 17th century in Germany.
Originally bred to assist on farms by driving livestock to market and guarding the farmer's property.
Document 3: A juniper berry is the female seed cone produced by the various species of junipers.
Juniper berries have been called the only spice derived from conifers.

topic.name: Military Aviation History
topic.description: The documents discuss various military aircraft and notable figures in aviation history during World War II and the Vietnam War.

Document 1: Mikoyan-Gurevich I-250 was a Soviet fighter aircraft developed as part of a crash program in 1944
the program was canceled in 1948 as obsolete.
Document 2: George Thomas Coker is a retired US Navy Commander, honored with the Navy Cross for his leadership as a prisoner of war during the Vietnam War
He was held as a prisoner of war in the 'Hanoi Hilton' for 6.5 years.
Document 3: The General Aircraft GAL.48 Hotspur was a military glider designed and built by the British company General Aircraft Ltd during World War II
The Hotspur was mainly relegated to training where it did excel.

topic.name: Sports Figures and Their Impact
topic.description: This topic encompasses notable athletes from various sports and their contributions to their respective games and teams.

Document 1: Steve Nash is a Canadian retired professional basketball player who played in the NBA.
He was an eight-time NBA All-Star and a two-time NBA Most Valuable Player.
Document 2: Bill Woodfull was an Australian cricketer and captain known for his dignified conduct during the bodyline series.
He was admired for his sportsmanship and ability to mould a successful team.
Document 3: Dick Padden was an American professional baseball player who played mainly as a second baseman.
He played nine seasons in Major League Baseball and was known for his post-career activities as a talent scout.

topic.name: Transportation Infrastructure
topic.description: The documents discuss various aspects of transportation infrastructure, including subway extensions and state highways.

Document 1: 7 Subway Extension is a subway extension of the New York City Subway's IRT Flushing Line.
The extension is a key part of the Hudson Yards Redevelopment Project.
Document 2: Down Street is a disused station on the London Underground.
The station was opened in 1907 by the Great Northern, Piccadilly and Brompton Railway.
Document 3: Maryland Route 7 is a collection of state highways in the U.S. state of Maryland.
The longest section of MD 7 begins at US 40 just east of the city of Baltimore.

topic.name: Political Figures and Activism
topic.description: This topic encompasses notable political figures and their contributions or actions within the context of governance and activism.

Document 1: John Baird served as Canada's Minister of Foreign Affairs
Baird held various cabinet positions in Canadian politics.
Document 2: Lolita Lebrón was a Puerto Rican nationalist
Lebrón led an assault on the United States House of Representatives in 1954.
Document 3: Harold A. Lafount served on the Federal Radio Commission
Lafount played an important part in the regulation of the broadcasting industry in the U.S.

topic.name: 19th Century Naval Ships
topic.description: The documents discuss various naval ships from the 19th century, focusing on their construction, specifications, and operational history.

Document 1: HMS Ocean was the last of the Royal Navy's four Prince Consort-class ironclads
The ship spent the bulk of her career on the China Station
Document 2: Andrei Pervozvanny was an Andrei Pervozvanny-class predreadnought battleship
The ship's construction was seriously extended by design changes as a result of the Russo-Japanese War
Document 3: HMS Lord Clyde was the name ship of the wooden-hulled Lord Clyde class of armoured frigates
Lord Clyde was initially assigned to the Channel Fleet in 1866

topic.name: Sporting Events and Competitions
topic.description: The documents discuss various sporting events and competitions, highlighting their history, participants, and outcomes.

Document 1: Tonga competed at the 2008 Summer Olympics
Tonga was represented by three athletes competing in two sports
Document 2: The Boat Race is a side-by-side rowing race between crews from the Universities of Oxford and Cambridge
Oxford won by two-and-a-half lengths
Document 3: Hydra the Revenge is a steel Floorless Coaster at Dorney Park
Its name comes from the Greek Mythology story where Hercules battled the Hydra

topic.name: State Highways in the U.S.
topic.description: The documents discuss various state highways in the United States, detailing their routes, characteristics, and connections to other roads.

Document 1: State Route 101 is a 21.811-mile long state highway located in the U.S. state of Utah.
SR-101 serves as a spur route into the Uinta-Wasatch-Cache National Forest.
Document 2: K-1 is a 13.363-mile state highway in Comanche County in the U.S. state of Kansas.
Its southern terminus is at the Oklahoma border south of Buttermilk.
Document 3: State Route 133 is a state highway in the U.S. state of California.
It connects SR 1 in Laguna Beach through the San Joaquin Hills with several freeways in Irvine.

topic.name: Film and Storytelling
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various films and their narratives, exploring themes of comedy, drama, and character development across different cultures.

Document 1: The Blues Brothers is a 1980 American musical crime comedy film directed by John Landis.
The story is a tale of redemption for paroled convict Jake and his brother Elwood.
Document 2: Gundamma Katha is a 1962 Indian Telugu-language comedy-drama film directed by Kamalakara Kameswara Rao.
It features N. T. Rama Rao, Akkineni Nageswara Rao, Savitri, and Jamuna in the lead roles.
Document 3: Kashimashi: Girl Meets Girl is a Japanese yuri manga series written by Satoru Akahori.
The story focuses on Hazumu Osaragi, a normal, albeit effeminate high school boy who is killed when an alien spaceship crash lands on him.

topic.name: Professional Wrestling History
topic.description: This topic encompasses the history and key figures of professional wrestling, focusing on events, wrestlers, and promotions.

Document 1: Sacrifice (2008) was a professional wrestling pay-per-view event produced by the Total Nonstop Action Wrestling (TNA) promotion.
The main event was a Three Way match for the TNA World Heavyweight Championship between then-champion Samoa Joe and challengers Kaz and Scott Steiner.
Document 2: Ashley Marie Massaro is an American former professional wrestler, valet, former WWE Diva and current general manager signed to World Independent Ladies Division Wrestling.
She is best known for her time with World Wrestling Entertainment (WWE) under the ring name Ashley (Massaro).
Document 3: Paul Michael Levesque, better known by his ring name Triple H, is an American professional wrestler, corporate executive, and actor.
He is the Executive Vice President of Talent, Live Events, & Creative of WWE.

topic.name: Television Series Episodes
topic.description: The documents discuss specific episodes from various American television series, highlighting their plots, character developments, and production details.

Document 1: eighth episode of the third season of the American mystery television series Veronica Mars
Veronica investigates and eventually finds out some startling information related to the radical feminist Lilith House
Document 2: sixth episode of the third season of the American television comedy series 30 Rock
Liz Lemon decides to help a charity for underprivileged children after her parents ditch their holiday plans
Document 3: Volume 1 of The X-Files Mythology collection is a DVD release containing selected episodes from the first to the third seasons
The episodes in the collection follow the investigations of paranormal-related cases, or X-Files, by FBI Special Agents Fox Mulder and Dana Scully

topic.name: Natural Phenomena and Ecosystems
topic.description: This topic encompasses various natural phenomena, including atmospheric dynamics, biological diversity, and geological formations in the environment.

Document 1: Jet streams are fast flowing , narrow , meandering air currents found in the upper atmosphere
The strongest jet streams are the polar jets, at 9 – 12 km ( 30,000 – 39,000 ft ) above sea level.
Document 2: Cicadas are a superfamily of insects in the order Hemiptera
They typically live in trees, feeding on sap, and laying their eggs in a slit in the bark.
Document 3: Davidson Seamount is a seamount ( underwater volcano ) located off the coast of Central California
The seamount is biologically diverse , with 237 species and 27 types of deep-sea coral having been identified.

topic.name: Spirituality and Philosophy
topic.description: The documents explore themes of spirituality, philosophical reflections on faith, and the development of cultural narratives.

Document 1: The book asserts that the countercultural themes in the ministry of Jesus, such as those of self-denial, are ignored by American Christians.
Claiborne and Haw make a compelling case that the church in America has become much too cozy with the state.
Document 3: The I Ching is an ancient divination text and the oldest of the Chinese classics.
The I Ching provides inspiration to the worlds of religion, psychoanalysis, business, literature, and art.

grossing over US $194.7 million from 60 shows

topic.name: Adventure and Puzzle Video Games
topic.description: The documents focus on various adventure and puzzle video games that emphasize exploration, storytelling, and problem-solving elements.

Document 1: Myst is a franchise centered on a series of adventure video games.
The player takes the role of an unnamed person referred to as the Stranger and assists Atrus by traveling to other Ages and solving puzzles.
Document 2: Tales of Symphonia is a Japanese role-playing game that follows Lloyd Irving.
As their journey progresses, they learn that saving Sylvarant endangers Tethe'alla, a world parallel to their own.
Document 3: The Potato Sack is the name of an alternate reality game (ARG) created by the Valve Corporation.
The game requiring players to find and solve a number of puzzles hidden within updates of the thirteen games.

topic.name: Historical Landmarks and Institutions
topic.description: This topic encompasses significant historical buildings and institutions that have cultural and architectural importance.

Document 1: St Mary 's Church is an active Anglican parish church located in Monk 's Lane , Acton , a village to the west of Nantwich , Cheshire , England.
Since 1967 it has been designated a Grade I listed building.
Document 2: The British Library Philatelic Collections is the national philatelic collection of the United Kingdom with over 8 million items from around the world.
It has become a primary research collection for British Empire and international history.
Document 3: Cape Moreton Light , also listed as North Point Range Rear Light , is an heritage-listed active lighthouse located on Cape Moreton.
It is the oldest lighthouse in Queensland, and the only one to be built by the New South Wales Government before the separation of Queensland.

topic.name: Historical Atlantic Hurricanes
topic.description: This topic covers significant hurricanes that occurred in the Atlantic Ocean, detailing their formation, impacts, and historical context.

Document 1: 1943 Surprise hurricane was the first hurricane to be entered by a reconnaissance aircraft.
the storm was considered the worst in Texas since the 1915 Galveston hurricane.
Document 2: The 1982 Florida subtropical storm was the only subtropical cyclone of the inactive 1982 Atlantic hurricane season.
The storm caused three fatalities and caused $10 million in damage.
Document 3: Hurricane Barbara was the second named tropical cyclone of the 1953 Atlantic hurricane season.
Damage from the hurricane was fairly minor, totaling around $1.3 million.

topic.name: Key Figures in Revolutionary Conflicts
topic.description: This topic encompasses influential military and political leaders during significant revolutionary periods in history, particularly the American Revolutionary War and World War II.

Document 1: Erich von Manstein was a German commander of the Wehrmacht during the Second World War.
He devised an innovative tactic known as the Sichelschnitt for the invasion of France.
Document 2: Thomas Hutchinson was a prominent Loyalist politician of the Province of Massachusetts Bay before the American Revolution.
Hutchinson was blamed for contributing to the tensions that led to the outbreak of the American Revolutionary War.
Document 3: Ethan Allen was a key figure in the American Revolutionary War and one of the founders of the U.S. state of Vermont.
He is best known for the capture of Fort Ticonderoga early in the American Revolutionary War.

topic.name: American Sports Achievements
topic.description: This topic encompasses the accomplishments and milestones of prominent American athletes across various sports disciplines.

Document 1: Aaron Peirsol is a three-time Olympian and seven-time Olympic medalist.
He holds the world record in the 100-meter and 200-meter backstroke events.
Document 2: Trevor Bayne won the 2011 Daytona 500, becoming the youngest winner of the event.
The race featured 16 cautions and 74 lead changes among 22 different drivers.
Document 3: Roy Roundtree was a leading receiver for the Michigan Wolverines football team.
He set Michigan's single-game receiving record with nine catches for 246 yards against Illinois.

topic.name: Hurricane Seasons and Impacts
topic.description: The documents discuss various Atlantic and Pacific hurricane seasons, detailing their formation, intensity, and impacts on affected regions.

Document 1: Hurricane Donna brought severe damage to the Lesser Antilles, the Greater Antilles, and the East Coast of the United States
Donna developed south of Cape Verde on August 29, spawned by a tropical wave
Document 2: The 2015 Atlantic hurricane season was a slightly below average season that produced twelve tropical cyclones
These dates historically describe the period each year when most tropical cyclones form in the Atlantic basin
Document 3: The 1988 Pacific hurricane season was a Pacific hurricane season that saw a below-average amount of tropical cyclones form
The season produced 23 tropical depressions, of which 15 attained tropical storm status

topic.name: Medieval Leadership and Governance
topic.description: This topic encompasses the roles and influences of prominent leaders and officials in medieval societies, particularly focusing on their governance, military actions, and familial legacies.

Document 1: Asad ibn Abdallah al-Qasri was a prominent official of the Umayyad Caliphate, serving twice as governor of Khurasan.
Asad tried to reconcile the local Soghdians to Muslim rule, initiated tax reforms to address the grievances of the native converts to Islam.
Document 2: Ragnall ua Ímair was a Viking leader who ruled Northumbria and the Isle of Man in the early 10th century.
His reign saw three issues of coinage, although this was perhaps done on the orders of Hrotheweard, Archbishop of York.
Document 3: Domhnall mac Raghnaill was a Hebridean noble in the late 12th- and early 13th-century.
He is the eponymous progenitor of Clan Donald (Clann Dhòmhnaill, 'Children of Donald').

topic.description: The documents explore various music genres, their origins, and influential artists, highlighting the intersection of different musical styles and cultural influences.

Document 1: Dubstep is a genre of electronic dance music that originated in South London

Document 2: 'It Is ' He ' ( Jai Sri Krishna )' is a song by English musician George Harrison

Musically, McCoy draws from various influences on the record, including hip-hop, reggae, and rock
